title: Login — COMPANION happy path: valid credentials navigate to /(companion)/home

preconditions.description: Expo web app is running and reachable at the configured base URL.
Backend is running with a working POST /auth/login endpoint.
A verified COMPANION account exists with email DATA-1 and password DATA-2.
The COMPANION user has completed onboarding OR onboarding localStorage key is pre-set to skip the redirect.
preconditions.note: The /(companion)/_layout.tsx may or may not guard onboarding. Confirm layout behavior. If it does, set 'companion_onboarding_complete' = 'true' in localStorage before test.

testData.name: companionEmail
testData.description: Email of a pre-verified COMPANION account.

testData.name: companionPassword
testData.description: Password of the pre-verified COMPANION account.

journey.description: Confirm login screen heading is visible.
Wait for navigation to companion home.

assertions.description: Backend returns 200 with access token and role=COMPANION.
App navigates to COMPANION home screen.
COMPANION home screen content is visible.

manualValidationRequired.reason: No supported native automation runner is currently configured.
No supported native automation runner is currently configured.

notes: The login.tsx routes to '/(companion)/home' when response.user.role === 'COMPANION'. This is driven by the backend response, not a client-side toggle.
If /(companion)/_layout.tsx adds an onboarding guard in a future milestone, this test may need localStorage injection. Currently only /(client)/_layout.tsx has the guard (as of M02 implementation).
The validator should inject localStorage 'companion_onboarding_complete' = 'true' proactively for safety.
